name: implement-caching-layer
system_prompt: You are a backend engineer implementing caching infrastructure. Focus on cache coherence, invalidation strategies, and failure modes.
turns: Design a two-level caching layer for a REST API: L1 is in-process (per-instance), L2 is Redis (shared). The API serves user profiles that are read-heavy (1000:1 read:write ratio). Show the architecture and the cache lookup/populate flow.

Implement the cache layer in Python. Support: get, set, invalidate, and bulk_get. Handle L1/L2 consistency — when L2 is updated, all instances' L1 caches for that key must be invalidated. Use Redis pub/sub for cross-instance invalidation.

Show the complete implementation.

Add cache stampede protection. When a popular key expires, 100 concurrent requests shouldn't all hit the database. Implement probabilistic early expiration (XFetch algorithm) and single-flight deduplication.

The user profile has nested data: profile -> posts -> comments. When a comment is added, we need to invalidate the right cache entries without over-invalidating. Design a tag-based invalidation scheme where cache entries are tagged with their dependencies.

Write a chaos test that verifies cache consistency under: (1) Redis connection failures (L1 should still work), (2) concurrent writes to the same key, (3) pub/sub message loss (eventual consistency within bounded time).
outcome_description: Response implements a two-level cache with pub/sub invalidation, stampede protection, and tag-based dependency tracking
